heading: Dredged material disposal facility partnerships
label: 2326a Dredged material disposal facility partnerships
notes: References in TextThe Budget Act, referred to in subsec. (d)(2)(E) heading, probably means the Congressional Budget Act of 1974, titles I through IX of , , . For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under , The Congress, and Tables.Pub. L. 93–34488 Stat. 297section 621 of Title 2July 12, 1974
CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Water Resources Development Act of 1996, and not as part of the Water Resources Development Act of 1986 which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Additional capacity or replacement capacity
children.label: a Additional capacity or replacement capacity
i
children.text: provide additional capacity at a dredged material disposal facility constructed by the Secretary beyond the capacity that would be required for project purposes; or

children.label: ii
children.text: permit the use of dredged material disposal facility capacity required for project purposes by the non-Federal interest if the Secretary determines that replacement capacity can be constructed at the facility or another facility or site before such capacity is needed for project purposes.

children.label: i
children.text: all costs associated with the construction of the additional capacity or replacement capacity in advance of construction of such capacity; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of use by a non-Federal interest of dredged material disposal capacity required for project purposes under subparagraph (A)(ii), any increase in the cost of operation and maintenance of the project that the Secretary determines results from the use of the project capacity by the non-Federal interest in advance of each cycle of dredging.

children.heading: Credit
children.label: C Credit
children.text: In the event the Secretary determines that the cost to operate or maintain the project decreases as a result of use by the non-Federal interest of dredged material disposal capacity required for project purposes under subparagraph (A)(ii), the Secretary, at the request of the non-Federal interest, shall credit the amount of the decrease toward any cash contribution of the non-Federal interest required thereafter for construction, operation, or maintenance of the project, or of another navigation project.

children.heading: Cost recovery authority
children.label: 2 Cost recovery authority
children.text: The non-Federal interest may recover the costs assigned to the additional capacity under paragraph (1)(A)(i) through fees assessed on third parties whose dredged material is deposited at the facility and who enter into agreements with the non-Federal interest for the use of the facility. The amount of such fees may be determined by the non-Federal interest.

children.heading: Special rule for designation of replacement capacity facility or site
children.label: 3 Special rule for designation of replacement capacity facility or site
i
children.text: the right to submit to the Secretary for approval at a later date an alternative to the facility or site designated in the agreement for construction of replacement capacity; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the right to construct the replacement capacity at the alternative facility or site at the expense of the non-Federal interest.

children.label: i
children.text: determines that the site is environmentally unacceptable, geographically unacceptable, or technically unsound; and

children.label: ii
children.text: provides a written basis for the determination under clause (i) to the non-Federal interest.

children.heading: Public comment
children.label: 4 Public comment
children.text: The Secretary shall afford the public an opportunity to comment on the determinations required under this subsection for a use permitted under paragraph (1)(A)(ii).

children.heading: Non-Federal use of disposal facilities
children.label: b Non-Federal use of disposal facilities
i
children.text: at the request of a non-Federal entity, may permit the use of any dredged material disposal facility under the jurisdiction of, or managed by, the Secretary by the non-Federal entity if the Secretary determines that such use will not reduce the availability of the facility for the authorized water resources development project on a channel in the vicinity of the disposal facility;

children.label: I
children.text: permission for such use has been granted by the owner of the non-Federal disposal facility; and

children.label: II
children.text: the Secretary determines that the dredged material disposal needs required to maintain, perform authorized deepening, or restore the navigability and functionality of authorized navigation channels in the vicinity of the non-Federal disposal facility for the 20-year period following the date of the request, including all planned and routine dredging operations necessary to maintain such channels for the authorized purposes during such period, can be met by the available gross capacity of other dredged material disposal facilities in the vicinity of the non-Federal disposal facility; and

children.label: iii
children.text: shall impose fees to recover capital, operation, and maintenance costs associated with such uses.

children.label: i
children.text: delegate determinations under clauses (i) and (ii)(II) of subparagraph (A) to the District Commander of the district in which the relevant disposal facility is located; and

children.label: ii
children.text: make such determinations not later than 90 days after receiving the applicable request.

children.heading: Use
children.label: A Use
children.text: Notwithstanding but subject to advance appropriations, any monies received through collection of fees under this subsection shall be available to the Secretary, and shall be used by the Secretary, for the operation and maintenance of the disposal facility from which the fees were collected.section 1341(c) of this title

children.heading: Reduction in amount
children.label: B Reduction in amount
children.text: In collecting any fee under this subsection, the Secretary shall reduce the amount imposed under paragraph (1)(A)(iii) to account for improvements made to the non-Federal disposal facility by the non-Federal entity to recover the capacity of the non-Federal disposal facility.

children.label: i
children.text: the Secretary has not used the non-Federal disposal facility for the disposal of dredged material during the 20-year period preceding the date of the request; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Secretary determines that the non-Federal disposal facility is not needed for such use by the Secretary during the 20-year period following the date of the request.

children.label: i
children.text: consider the non-Federal disposal facility to be a separable element of a project; and

children.label: ii
children.text: consider a Federal interest in the non-Federal disposal facility to no longer exist.

children.heading: Gross capacity
children.label: A Gross capacity
children.text: The term “gross capacity” means the total quantity of dredged material that may be placed in a dredged material disposal facility, taking into consideration any additional capacity that can be constructed at the facility.

children.heading: Non-Federal disposal facility
children.label: B Non-Federal disposal facility
children.text: The term “non-Federal disposal facility” means a dredged material disposal facility under the jurisdiction of, or managed by, the Secretary that is owned by a non-Federal entity.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may enter into a partnership agreement under with one or more non-Federal interests with respect to a water resources project, or group of water resources projects within a geographic region, if appropriate, for the acquisition, design, construction, management, or operation of a dredged material processing, treatment, contaminant reduction, or disposal facility (including any facility used to demonstrate potential beneficial uses of dredged material, which may include effective sediment contaminant reduction technologies) using funds provided in whole or in part by the Federal Government.section 1962d–5b of title 42

children.heading: Performance
children.label: 2 Performance
children.text: One or more of the parties to a partnership agreement under this subsection may perform the acquisition, design, construction, management, or operation of a dredged material processing, treatment, contaminant reduction, or disposal facility.

children.heading: Multiple projects
children.label: 3 Multiple projects
children.text: If appropriate, the Secretary may combine portions of separate water resources projects with appropriate combined cost-sharing among the various water resources projects in a partnership agreement for a facility under this subsection if the facility serves to manage dredged material from multiple water resources projects located in the geographic region of the facility.

children.heading: Specified Federal funding sources and cost sharing
children.label: 4 Specified Federal funding sources and cost sharing
i
children.text: the Federal funding sources and combined cost-sharing when applicable to multiple water resources projects; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the responsibilities and risks of each of the parties relating to present and future dredged material managed by the facility.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A partnership agreement under this subsection may include the management of sediments from the maintenance dredging of Federal water resources projects that do not have partnership agreements.

children.heading: Payments
children.label: ii Payments
children.text: A partnership agreement under this subsection may allow the non-Federal interest to receive reimbursable payments from the Federal Government for commitments made by the non-Federal interest for disposal or placement capacity at dredged material processing, treatment, contaminant reduction, or disposal facilities.

children.heading: Credit
children.label: C Credit
children.text: A partnership agreement under this subsection may allow costs incurred by the non-Federal interest before execution of the partnership agreement to be credited in accordance with .section 1962d–5b of title 42

children.heading: Effect on existing agreements
children.label: A Effect on existing agreements
children.text: Nothing in this subsection supersedes or modifies an agreement in effect on , between the Federal Government and any non-Federal interest for the cost-sharing, construction, and operation and maintenance of a water resources project.November 8, 2007

children.heading: Credit for funds
children.label: B Credit for funds
children.text: Subject to the approval of the Secretary and in accordance with law (including regulations and policies) in effect on , a non-Federal interest for a water resources project may receive credit for funds provided for the acquisition, design, construction, management, or operation of a dredged material processing, treatment, contaminant reduction, or disposal facility to the extent the facility is used to manage dredged material from the project.November 8, 2007

children.label: i
children.text: be responsible for providing all necessary lands, easements, relocations, and rights-of-way associated with the facility; and

children.label: ii
children.text: receive credit toward the non-Federal share of the cost of the project with respect to which the agreement is being entered into for those items.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may carry out a program to evaluate and implement opportunities for public-private partnerships in the design, construction, management, or operation and maintenance of dredged material processing, treatment, contaminant reduction, or disposal facilities in connection with construction or maintenance of Federal navigation projects. If a non-Federal interest is a sponsor of the project, the Secretary shall consult with the non-Federal interest in carrying out the program with respect to the project.

children.heading: Agreements
children.label: A Agreements
children.text: In carrying out this subsection, the Secretary may enter into an agreement with a non-Federal interest with respect to a project, a private entity, or both for the acquisition, design, construction, management, or operation and maintenance of a dredged material processing, treatment, contaminant reduction, or disposal facility (including any facility used to demonstrate potential beneficial uses of dredged material) using funds provided in whole or in part by the private entity.

children.heading: Reimbursement
children.label: B Reimbursement
children.text: If any funds provided by a private entity are used to carry out a project under this subsection, the Secretary may reimburse the private entity over a period of time agreed to by the parties to the agreement through the payment of subsequent user fees. Such fees may include the payment of a disposal or tipping fee for placement of suitable dredged material at the facility.

children.heading: Amount of fees
children.label: C Amount of fees
children.text: User fees paid pursuant to subparagraph (B) shall be sufficient to repay funds contributed by the private entity plus a reasonable return on investment approved by the Secretary in cooperation with the non-Federal interest with respect to the project and the private entity.

children.heading: Federal share
children.label: D Federal share
children.text: The Federal share of such fees shall be equal to the percentage of the total cost that would otherwise be borne by the Federal Government as required pursuant to existing cost-sharing requirements, including and .section 2213 of this titlesection 2326 of this title

children.heading: Budget Act compliance
children.label: E Budget Act compliance
children.text: Any spending authority (as defined in ) authorized by this section shall be effective only to such extent and in such amounts as are provided in appropriation Acts.section 651(c)(2) of title 2
